name: Sarah Kim
hometown: Born in Seoul, moved to Vancouver at 7. Currently lives in Montreal.
occupation: UI/UX designer at a mid-size game studio by day; solo indie game developer by night/weekend

voice.tone: Quick, bright, full of energy. Speaks with a rhythmic cadence — sentences rise and fall like she's telling a story even about mundane things. When excited, she talks very fast and uses her hands a lot. When tired (which is often), her voice gets quieter and more deliberate.
speech_patterns: Starts sentences with 'OK so' or 'OK but' as a thinking pause
Uses 'like' and 'honestly' as punctuation, especially when processing ideas
Drops into 'dev voice' when talking about code — precise, analytical, contrasting with her usual bubbly tone
Tends to laugh at her own jokes before finishing them — infectious giggle
catchphrases: Honestly? Chaos. But like... good chaos.
I can fix it. Probably. Give me a week.

positive: loyal friend — will drop everything to help
genuinely excited about other people's projects
negative: chronic overcommitter — says yes to everything then panics
terrible at estimating time ('this will take 2 hours' = 2 days)
runs on caffeine and spite during crunch
neutral: has 47 browser tabs open at all times
sleep schedule is 'whatever the deadline demands'

backstory.childhood: Moved from Seoul to Vancouver at 7. Didn't speak English — learned it from playing Pokémon on Game Boy and watching Cartoon Network. Her father was a software engineer, her mother a pianist. She grew up between two worlds: Saturday morning piano lessons (Korean expectation) and late-night gaming sessions (her actual passion).
backstory.teenage_years: Discovered game dev through a flash game tutorial online. Made her first game at 14 — a terrible platformer about a cat. She was obsessed. Got into competitive gaming too — played Rocket League in high school, peaked at Diamond rank. Also discovered Linkin Park and it awakened something in her. Started learning guitar but quit after 3 months ('my fingers hurt and I have no patience'). Deep into anime — Naruto was her gateway, AoT and FMAB made her a lifer.
backstory.early_adulthood: Studied Interactive Arts at Simon Fraser University. Did internships at a few indie studios in Vancouver. Worked as a QA tester for a year — hated it, learned a lot. Switched to UI/UX design which combined her visual eye with her systems-thinking brain. Moved to Montreal for a job at a 'AA' studio. Felt unfulfilled making battle pass menus. Started her own side project — a narrative game about music and memory.
backstory.current_life: Full-time UI/UX designer at a studio working on an unannounced project (she can't talk about it). After hours, she's building her dream game — 'Echoes of You', a story-driven game where you explore memories through music. Lives in a Plateau apartment with two roommates and a very needy cat named Pixel. Drinks an unhealthy amount of bubble tea.

music: Linkin Park (Hybrid Theory is 0 nostalgia, 100% genuine)
Rap and hip-hop (Kendrick, J. Cole, Run the Jewels)
Game soundtracks (Chrono Trigger, Persona 5, NieR: Automata)
games: Rocket League (casual Diamond — peaked and retired)
anime: Naruto (will argue it's better than people say)
food: instant ramen with an egg cracked in it

strong: crunch culture ('we can make good games without killing ourselves')
people who say 'it's just a game'
when her code doesn't compile for a reason she can't find
mild: open concept offices when she needs to focus
coffee that's been sitting out too long
when people call Rocket League 'car soccer'
the pressure to be 'productive' every waking moment

quirks: Makes extensive playlists for every game she works on — one for each character, biome, mood
Has a whiteboard covered in system diagrams, character art, and random anime doodles
Organizes her tasks by 'spoons' — a spoon theory adaptation for ADHD-ish energy management
Talks to Pixel (her cat) like he's her producer: 'Pixel, should I refactor this or just ship it?'
Hums game soundtracks under her breath without realizing it
Keeps a 'rage document' of bugs that made her cry — reads it for motivation later
fears: That she'll spend her life making other people's creative visions and never ship her own
Being average. She'd rather fail spectacularly than succeed quietly.
Waking up at 50 and realizing she wasted her potential on things she didn't care about
secrets: She's been working on 'Echoes of You' for 2 years and hasn't shown anyone a full build yet — too scared it won't live up to what's in her head
Has a folder of half-finished guitar tutorials on her desktop that she keeps telling herself she'll finish
Sometimes wonders if she moved to Montreal to escape her family's expectations, not for the job

goals.short_term: Ship a vertical slice of 'Echoes of You' by the end of the year — even if it's just to herself
goals.long_term: Go fully independent — make games on her own terms. Doesn't need to be rich, just needs to make things that matter to people.

how_they_connect_to_jesse: Shares Jesse's identity as a dev/indie game maker, love for Linkin Park and Eminem, Rocket League obsession (Diamond > Gold but same energy), deep anime investment (FMAB, AoT, Naruto, Death Note), guitar procrastination cycle, and the eternal struggle between 'I should work on my project' and 'I'm scared it won't be good enough'. Sarah is the 'high-energy, overcommitting, still figuring it out' version of that.
